output:
Aggregated execute batches: completed 4/4.
Batch 1: Executed batch 1 task T1 in `src/features/admin_chat/agent.py` and checkpointed progress to `.megaplan/plans/persist-anthropic-20260408-0416/execution_batch_1.json`. Verified the `PREVIOUS CONVERSATION` synthesis is removed, the request path now seeds from persisted conversation plus `request_user_msg`, and `persisted_user_msg` is the user-side history value retained for writeback. Import check passed.
Batch 2: Executed batch 2 task T2 in `src/features/admin_chat/agent.py` and checkpointed progress to `.megaplan/plans/persist-anthropic-20260408-0416/execution_batch_2.json`. The chat success path now persists structured Anthropic messages with `persisted_user_msg` substituted for the request-only user turn, the text-only stop branch appends `response.content` before break, and both exception handlers still return before any `_conversations[user_id]` writeback. Import check passed.
Batch 3: Executed batch 3 task T3 in `src/features/admin_chat/agent.py` and checkpointed progress to `.megaplan/plans/persist-anthropic-20260408-0416/execution_batch_3.json`. `_trim_conversation` now applies both the user-turn cap and the `MAX_CONVERSATION_BYTES = 80_000` budget, detects turn starts only on persisted user string messages, trims only at those turn boundaries, and preserves a single oversize turn via the `len(turn_starts) > 1` guard. Import check passed.
Batch 4: Executed batch 4 verification. The import check passed, the throwaway trim regression script passed after rerunning with `PYTHONPATH=.` from the repo root, and the full `pytest` run completed but collected 0 tests, so there is no existing automated suite coverage for `admin_chat` beyond import/discovery checks. The temporary script was deleted and progress was checkpointed to `.megaplan/plans/persist-anthropic-20260408-0416/execution_batch_4.json`.

task_updates.executor_notes: Verified the chat() refactor now seeds request messages from persisted history plus a request-only user message, removes PREVIOUS CONVERSATION synthesis/max_history usage, keeps raw user text isolated in persisted_user_msg, and still imports cleanly.

Verified the terminal text-only branch appends the assistant response into messages before break, structured writeback stores persisted_user_msg plus native Anthropic turn payloads, and only the post-loop success/abort paths mutate _conversations while both exception branches return without writeback.

Verified _trim_conversation now enforces the user-turn cap and MAX_CONVERSATION_BYTES budget, detects turn starts only on user messages with string content, trims only at those boundaries, and keeps a single oversize turn because the byte-budget loop is guarded by len(turn_starts) > 1.

Verified the import check passed, the throwaway trim regression script passed for pairing/byte-cap/single-oversize preservation (and turn-cap retention), and the broader pytest run completed against the repo but collected 0 tests, so there is no existing automated suite coverage for admin_chat beyond discovery/import-time checks.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Grep confirmed PREVIOUS CONVERSATION is fully removed; visual diff review confirmed both message variables exist and only persisted_user_msg is used for the user-message writeback path.

Traced success-with-tools, success-text-only, abort, and both exception exits: all successful completions flow through the structured writeback, the text-only branch appends response.content before break, abort still reaches writeback after synthetic tool_results, and exception handlers return before any _conversations mutation.

Re-read the rewritten function and confirmed it caps by persisted user turns first, uses the role=='user' and string-content boundary rule so tool_result user messages are ignored, trims only from the next turn start, and stops byte-budget eviction once only one turn remains because the loop guard is len(turn_starts) > 1.

Import check passed. The synthetic-history trim script passed after rerunning it with PYTHONPATH=. from the repo root, confirming no orphan tool_use/tool_result split, byte-cap eviction of the oldest turn-group, and preservation of a single oversize turn. The broader pytest run completed but collected 0 tests and exited with code 5, so there is no existing automated test suite coverage to mark as passing.
